evidence: The amber probe arrow plus the climbing lilac score bars carry the beat; the periwinkle candidates and labels read as steady supporting context.
Only one probe arrow, a group of four candidate arrows, and a matching row of four score bars appear — well within the budget.
Labels A/B/C/D sit at each arrow tip and repeat beside each corresponding score bar; no legend or corner key is used.
On-screen text is limited to candidate letters and integer bar readouts (-3, 0, 3, 4, etc.), each annotating a visible object.
Candidate letters and the numeric bar readouts are rendered at readable size against the dark sky.
The arrow fan and the full row of four score bars sit inside the frame with margin on all sides.
Candidate labels are nudged outward past their tips, so even the sweeping amber probe near B/C never overlaps a label, and bar numbers stay clear.
The probe is amber (attention), the candidate arrows periwinkle (observed), and the score bars lilac — consistent with token semantics.
Every element — probe, candidate arrows, labels, score bars, and readouts — carries propositional content with no decorative filler.
The amber probe sweeps from toward-A (A=4 highest) to toward-C/D (C=4, D=3), the four lilac bars climb and dip with the angle, and integers land on the bars as promised.
Each score bar sits under its labeled candidate in matching left-to-right order, keeping claim and object together.
The sequence builds continuously from a bare origin dot to arrows, then bars filling and probe rotating across frames.
Generous negative space separates the arrow fan above from the score-bar row below with a clear top-to-bottom scan path.
